Devyn is a breathtakingly beautiful black cat with a glossy midnight coat and striking eyes that reflect every bit of her intelligence and curiosity. At just 2 years old, she already carries herself with the elegance and quiet confidence of a feline queen.

Curious by nature, Devyn loves to explore her surroundings and observe from a safe, elevated spot. She’s fascinated by the world around her, but she prefers to approach everything-especially people-in her own time. Devyn has some trust issues and can be cautious with new humans, but she’s making steady progress. Her foster mom is gently earning her trust day by day, and Devyn is showing more and more of her sweet, inquisitive personality.

Devyn isn’t shy about expressing her feelings. She is very vocal when she’s unhappy or uncomfortable, making her a cat who communicates clearly and honestly. Her ideal home is one where her boundaries are respected and her cues are understood.

She does get along well with both dogs and other cats, as long as they respect her space and don’t overwhelm her. Calm, patient companions-furry or human-will help her shine.

Devyn is looking for someone who sees the beauty in letting a relationship unfold naturally. With patience, understanding, and love, she’ll reward you with trust, companionship, and her own special brand of charm.

The best way to show interest in an animal is to submit an adoption application by visiting our website (www.rescuenetworkmn.org).

Adoption fee includes spay/neuter, all age-appropriate vaccinations (distemper, rabies, and bordetella), heartworm and flea/tick preventatives, heartworm test (dogs), FIV/FeLV test (cats), dewormed, and microchip implant (including registration).

Rescue Network is strictly foster based which means all adoptable animals are in a home and there cannot be a meet until an application is submitted and approved. We have no way of knowing the breeds in the animals. All breeds listed are best guess.

Hello! I'm Beau! I'm a very sweet boy with a unique feature. I'm a tripod! But don't let that fool you, I'm even more playful as ever! I love to go on walks and be outside. I even enjoy the water! I am house and kennel trained, and I love to hang out with the other dogs in my foster home. Sometimes we will play, but we all like to relax together too! While I get along with the dogs in my foster home, I do need slower intros with other dogs to help me feel comfortable.

I enjoy puzzle toys and dog safe chews.  I do try to play with the cats but they don't want to play, so a home without cats or dog saavy cats would be best. While I am a playful and adventerous boy, I also like to snuggle sometimes. Apartment life is too overwhelming for me, so I need a house, preferably with a fenced in yard, to call my forever home. However, the fenced in yard is not a dealbreaker since I enjoy going on walks.

I do have anxiety and allergies, so I take meds for them. But don't worry, I do really well taking them!

 Are you my perfect match?

Hi, I’m Pepper! 🐾

I’m an 8-year-old bundle of love wrapped in the softest fur and the kindest heart. My foster family says I’m one of the sweetest souls you’ll ever meet. Once I get to know you, I’ll be your shadow, your snuggle buddy, and your biggest fan. I love giving kisses, curling up next to you, and being the center of your world - because to me, you’ll be the center of mine.

At first, I can be a little cautious when meeting new people. I just need a little time to feel safe, but once I do, my whole heart belongs to you. When you earn my trust, you’ll see my goofy, affectionate side - the part of me that loves to play, lean in for hugs, and make you laugh with my big, silly “lap dog” personality (all 80 pounds of it!).

I currently live with another female dog who’s 5 years old, and we have so much fun together! We love to play and chase - though sometimes I forget my own strength. I also love my naps and have been told my snoring is absolutely adorable. It’s just one of my many charms! I adore toys (maybe a little too much!), so I prefer to have my own stash to keep things peaceful. I’m also a big fan of treats, and I’m happy to share as long as everyone gets one! I’m mostly house-trained and try so hard to be a good girl. If I ever have an accident, it’s never on purpose. I always feel bad and want to make it right because I really do try my best to make my humans proud.

My foster parents say they truly don’t want to let me go, but they know there’s a perfect family out there who will love me unconditionally and make me the center of their world. I still have so much life to live and even more love to give. I’m full of joy, warmth, and loyalty and I’m just waiting for someone who will see me for the beautiful, loving companion I am. If you’re looking for a gentle, devoted, and endlessly loving best friend to share your heart and home with, I promise - I’m your girl. All I need is a chance, and I’ll spend the rest of my life showing you just how special forever can be. ❤️

Hello, I’m Zola! I am super adorable and ready to find my forever home! I’m not going to lie, I was in pretty rough shape when Rescue Network rescued me. But now, I am living the good life! My foster mom says I’m as sweet as can be and full of personality!

I am kennel trained, potty trained, and I love having a cozy spot to nap. I’m super playful, affectionate, and guaranteed to make you laugh! I love giving kisses and playing with other dogs, cats, and kids! I love playing with toys too, but I’m not the best at sharing toys with other dogs yet. But I’m working on it! I love to talk and sing to my foster mom, so if you’re looking for a vocal girl with a hilarious personality - I’m the one for you!

I am looking for a loving family who will continue helping me grow and show me all the good things life has to offer. If you’re ready for endless love, puppy kisses, and wagging tails, I would love to meet you!

Icy Pop is a typical, fun-loving, energetic dog! She would be an ideal companion for someone seeking a younger dog to grow with and cherish as a lifelong friend. Prepare to be spoiled with cuddles and face kisses!

Shy at first, she ended up loving our three other dogs. They wrestle and run each other out in the backyard! When not playing with her foster siblings, Icy Pop likes to chew on her toys, LOVES to play ball or simply take a relaxing nap by you. She is treat-motivated and knows how to sit and we are working on “lay down”.

Icy Pop sleeps through out the night with us just fine, and is a super big cuddler! She is very well potty trained. She has a little kennel anxiety and uses a heavy-duty crate (which foster parents will provide), but she has been making progress over the past month. We have been testing leaving her out and about with our other dogs for shorts amounts of time and has been improving with that also, but still has some puppy tendencies.

Although we do not have kids, I wouldn’t see her being a problem around them. She’s great with large groups of people and our neighborhood friends. This girl just wants to be loved and pampered!

Meet Red Lobster(a.k.a. Roxy)

Red Lobster is a sweet, loving girl with a fun and energetic personality. She thrives in an active home where she has space to run and play—especially with other dogs her size. She’s also wonderful with kids of all ages, making her a great family companion.

While Red Lobster can be a little shy at first, she just needs a bit of time to warm up. Once she does, she becomes incredibly loyal and loves to stay close to her people—wherever you are, she’ll want to be right by your side.

She is house-trained and ready to transition smoothly into her new home. Because of her natural instincts, she would do best in a home without chickens or small animals.

If you’re looking for an affectionate, playful, and devoted companion, Red Lobster could be the perfect fit!

Meet this beautiful girl who's ready to find her forever family! At around 1 year old, she's the perfect mix of playful energy and cuddle-bug sweetness.

She's potty trained, walks wonderfully on a leash, and absolutely loves spending time with her people. Whether you're heading out for a walk or settling in for a movie night, she's happiest right by your side.

She's great with dogs, cats, and kids, making her a wonderful addition to many different types of families. One thing she does ask for is a little personal space when it comes to food, treats, toys, and bones. She prefers to enjoy those on her own, so feeding separately and picking up high-value items when other pets are around helps everyone stay happy. Outside of those moments, she's a loving and friendly companion.

She's not a big fan of being kenneled and would much rather be out with her family, soaking up all the love and attention she can get. If you're looking for a loyal best friend who will shower you with affection and be your constant companion, she might just be the perfect match for you!

Are you looking for a super goofy, kinda crazy, a little stubborn, very loving pup with a touch of shyness?  Then you have found your pup!

Maple is all of the above. Not a day goes by when we don't laugh at what she does, or question what she does (or how she does it). She is more kangaroo than dog most of the time and she doesn't seem like she has any intention of changing that.

Maple is 99% potty trained. She does still have an accident here and there if you aren't paying attention to let her out. She is not a fan of car rides. Short rides she does okay, but she likes to turn into a waterfall after about 10 minutes.

Maple is great with other dogs and she will need another dog in the home to help her adjust and feel comfortable. She is also great with cats. She does play with them as our cats enjoy playing with her.

She does well with kids, but would be best in a home without young kids, due to her tending to be more on the skiddish side, as well as her jumping.

Maple is a high energy pup who needs a fenced in yard to safely get her frequent zoomies out. She does pretty well on a leash, but she won't potty while she is leashed.

She loves to play with leaves and recently discovered that she thoroughly enjoys the pool and the hose. Watching her enjoy the simple things brings so much joy and laughter everyday.

While she definitely has her stubborn tendencies and little quirks, I know there is a perfect family for her out there that will love her just the way she is.

Chrissy is a gorgeous tabby brimming with intelligence and curiosity. Because of her past, she's now in the process of learning that people, consistent food, and a clean space are pretty great. Chrissy gives off the air of confidence and mild disdain for her underlings that many great cats possess - it takes a lot to impress her! This will make winning over her love and affection even more rewarding for the humans who choose to make her part of their family. She hates to admit it but she already loves the neck scratches she allows while eating her favorite treats. There may even be belly rubs in her future for the person who is willing to earn her trust. Chrissy is athletic and independent but tolerant of other cats. She would do well as a solo feline or in a multi-cat household.

Meet Jillian (a.k.a. Junie)!

If you are looking for a pup with a ton of personality and a lot of love, then Jillian is your girl.

With big amber eyes and a uniquely painted coat, Jillian is a little showstopper. She loves all humans she meets and is a huge sweetheart, but does need continued boundary work as she thinks the best way to let others know she is a fan is to get right up into their personal space! While she is smart and has picked up on cues and commands very quickly, ongoing training is still needed.

Jillian is now crate trained and has done well being crated for longer periods of time while her foster is at work. When crated, she tends to do better in a high-traffic area so she can observe what is going on around her and not miss out. The only time she cries anymore when crated is because she has a serious case of FOMO and just wants to join in the fun. While Jillian has not been left alone in the house outside the crate for long periods of time, during shorter bouts, she has done really well and usually just ends up lounging in the living room or sunroom.

Jillian is potty-trained and does very well with potty prompts once outside. She did clear our 3-foot fence at one point and had a nice little run in our neighbor’s yard so is currently on a lead even with a fenced-in backyard. As an almost 2-year-old, Jillian still has a lot of youthful energy and looks forward to going for her walk first thing in the morning. And while she has made a lot of progress over the last few months, Jillian does have a high prey drive and exhibits some excitable reactivity as a result.

Jillian is normally taken on a couple walks a day to work on her leash skills and she is catching on really quickly - we are learning to “leave it”, “wait”, and “eyes” (when needing her attention); she tends to crowd and pull on the leash so we are also working on side-by-side walking and pressure commands with the leash. She also still struggles with off-leash recall but with continued patience and consistency, will no doubt continue to improve. Overall, she would greatly benefit from working with a trainer as it appears that most things are still very new to her.

While Jillian initially did well with the resident dog, more recently, she seems to be struggling to read different social cues and has shown some aggressive reactivity. As a result, we have been keeping her on a lead or in the crate as a precaution when both dogs are in a shared space in the house. Since Jillian was found as a stray, it is hard to know her history with other dogs; she does express high interest in playing with other dogs but will require high levels of monitoring.

In just a short amount of time, Jillian has learned both the verbal and nonverbal signals for “sit”, “wait”, “hands in”, “stay” and “lie down”. She transports well and loves her weekly outdoor adventures at Fort Snelling. Overall, Jillian's ideal home is with an active family or person who is patient, does not have any other pets, is in a house (she does have a heavy bark!) with a high fenced-in yard. If you are ready for a lively spirit, funny personality, and unconditional companionship - look no further than Jillian!

Marigold (Tilly) is a sweet puppy with a gentle spirit and a loyal heart. She thrives on routine and loves being close to her people, enjoying cozy spots and quiet moments where she feels safe. She has been thriving with a consistent routine; it’s almost as if she has an internal clock that tells her what comes next.

Tilly would love a home that enjoys hikes and adventure walks. She’s a semi-active girl who is currently working on managing her excitement, and she’s eager to learn. She is treat-motivated and very gentle when taking treats from hands, which makes training both fun and rewarding. She’s also practicing patience in new environments and will work on it during upcoming outings, such as trips to the pet store.

Due to her past, Tilly does have food insecurities, so she does best with consistent mealtimes and respectful boundaries around food. She settles quickly, often burrowing under a blanket in her kennel, and sleeps peacefully near her foster family at night.

Tilly may do best as the only dog, where she can fully relax and feel secure. She would flourish in a calm, loving home that offers stability, patience, affection, and gentle guidance.

If you’re looking for a loyal companion who enjoys both adventure and cozy downtime, Marigold (Tilly) is ready to meet you.

I’m quick to warm up to small humans first, they’re the BEST & my absolute favorite. Gibson is a suspected terrier/cattle dog/husky mix with a big personality and an even bigger brain. This smart boy thrives when he has something to do and loves engaging with people through play, training, and toys.

Gibson has medium-to-high energy and enjoys active homes where he can burn off steam and keep his mind busy. He especially loves toys and interactive play, and he isn’t shy about showing his strength and enthusiasm when the fun starts.

He’s curious about other dogs and cats, and tends to be confident and a bit domineering, so introductions and continued guidance will help him succeed. Gibson plays hard and appreciates companions who understand his energetic style.

Because of his strength and play intensity, Gibson would do best in a home with older kids or adults only (no small children).

With the right structure, exercise, and someone who appreciates a smart, driven, playful dog, Gibson will make a loyal and entertaining companion.

Meet Buttercup!

Buttercup is a sweet, gentle pup who can be a little shy and reserved when meeting new people, but once she feels safe she becomes very loving and loyal to her people.

Buttercup has low-to-moderate energy and enjoys both playtime and cuddles. She loves playing fetch and will happily bring the ball back so you can throw it again. She’s also extremely treat and food motivated, which makes training fun and rewarding. Belly rubs, snacks, and greeting her people when they come home are some of her favorite things.

She is good with other dogs, but can be reactive when she sees them from a distance and does best with slow introductions. Buttercup doesn’t need another dog in the home, but a calm, friendly dog could help build her confidence.

Buttercup is afraid of men and will need a patient home that allows her time to warm up. Because she isn’t familiar with toddlers, she would do best with older children. She sleeps well in her crate at night and learned potty training quickly.

Buttercup cannot live with cats.

She’s a very sweet, cuddly girl who has clearly had a difficult past and just needs a patient family who will give her the love and security she deserves.

She does great on the leash, no pulling and she’s able to greet other dogs on the leash nicely. She’s fully potty trained and does well in the crate.

Hayden’s foster has 2 kids (4 & 7 years old) and she does great with them. She does well with the other 2 dogs in the house, and loves to run and wrestle with them.

If you're looking for a smart, loyal companion who loves toys and is always down to play, meet Ari!

This handsome Border Collie/German Shepherd mix is fully house-trained, kennel-trained, and has excellent leash manners.

Ari is a fun-loving guy who needs a little time to warm up to other dogs, but once he’s comfortable, it’s a non-stop play fest. Because he has that classic herding dog intelligence and high energy, he can get a bit overwhelmed by the quick movements and unpredictability of toddlers, so he's looking for a home with no small kids where he can truly shine.

Fully neutered, microchipped, and up-to-date on vaccinations, Ari is ready to bring a whole lot of love to his perfect forever home!

Foxglove (a.k.a. “Foxy”) is 7-years-old but don’t let that turn you away from this lovely, sweetheart with a gentle and loyal soul. 

Summary of her wonderful qualities:

*House-trained (not one accident)

*Good with other dogs

*Good with older kids (unknown if she has been around young kids)

*Minimally vocal / not "barky"

*Great listener & loyal

*Not destructive

*Enjoys being outside

*Fantastic leash manners

*Professional cuddler & affectionate (no licking though)

*Gently takes treats

*Gladly accepts belly rubs

*No resource guarding

Foxy is incredibly loyal and much prefers the companionship/attention of humans & receiving pets than playing with toys. She is very attentive to her human and listens well. She will stay nearby and appears to be genuinely interested in everything her people are doing. That being said, due to her loyalty & strong sense of pack mentality, she has escaped kennels and yards to try to go find her human if they leave. But, if they are with her, then she will not try to escape and is happy. Therefore, a fenced in yard might be best for her.

Foxglove is one who will love you dearly and has that classic shepherd loyalty. She is a gem with lots of love! Give her a chance and apply to meet (and/or adopt) her!

*She had some teeth extracted so she has a nice, derpy smile too haha.

Outside brings so much stimulation with squirrels and birds to chase, sticks to chew on or drag around the yard and digging for treasures in the ground.  He loves to explore. Pixel would do well as an only dog if he has an active owner or family to keep him busy.  He likes and plays well with other dogs. Pixel is a typical puppy with puppy energy and enthusiasm.

Are you looking for an athletic, lap-sized, snuggly, lover of life! Meet Utica! When not laying in the sun, or snuggling with her people, this girl LOVES action!

Hi, I'm Norman! My foster mom scooped me up when she heard how I came in to rescue.. I was hanging around the hospital, trying to find some food, when some big dogs came at me and chased me away. Luckily, a nice lady who worked there came to help me. I've adjusted to the good life in my foster home really well. There are three other pups here, which I absolutely LOVE! I am learning a lot from the older ones, and I just love to play with the other puppy. My foster mom says my play style is somewhat rough, but I prefer to call it exuberant. A couple weeks ago, I was having a great time playing with my foster brothers, until one of them stepped on me and broke my toe! I am doing my best to heal, but let me tell you it is BORING. I will be all healed up in late July or early August and I can't wait to get back to running around without a care in the world!

I'm an energetic guy who is always up for an adventure! I am very food-motivated and happy to have any snack - even the healthy ones! I eat and sleep in my crate, but if I am in there outside of those times and you are home, I will tell you how I feel about it! My foster mom tells me her favorite thing about me is my ears, because they are a good mix of floppy and standing up. If you think we might be a good fit, apply to meet me! My foster mom would be happy to tell you all about me!

From my foster mom: Chat GPT estimates that he will be around 50 lbs fully grown, so if you are looking for a pup with the looks of a big dog, but on the smaller side, Norman is for you! I think he would do best in a home with another dog! Both for his happiness, and to allow him to burn some of his boundless energy off easily. He has lived with small children before, and his energy level and love for play chomping did not work out well. He is very sweet and gentle with children when meeting them, but it may be best for him to go to a home with older children only. I am hoping he grows out of some of that, but only time will tell! Norman is a great dog and will be perfect for someone who is very active. When he finally settles down he is a great snuggler!

Adopt Grimsby. Because every queen deserves her castle. 🐾

Shalla is an old soul with a youthful heart. There’s a gentle wisdom about her that’s apparent the moment you see her—but step closer, and she’ll happily surprise you with joyful hops and rapid‑fire kisses. She’s the kind of dog who reminds you that some hearts stay forever young.

Shalla has spent much of her life being a devoted mama, and now it’s her turn to be cared for. And care for her you will—because she is stunning. From her beautifully lined eyes to her glowing golden coat, she turns heads wherever she goes.

We aren’t quite sure of her breed, but she has the majestic face of a wolf paired with big, elegant paws—though she’s not especially tall. Her coat is thick, sleek, and surprisingly easy to maintain considering how full it is.

Shalla would thrive in a home with people around most of the time. She loves kids and enjoys being part of the action. She’s polite with other dogs—she likes them well enough but doesn’t necessarily need a canine companion to be happy. What she does need is togetherness. Shalla doesn’t love being alone, and adopters should be prepared to use a heavy‑duty crate and possibly medication when away from home.

Aside from that, she is an amazing, well‑behaved dog who simply wants to be close to her people. And yes—we know you’re wondering—she has had zero accidents.

If you’re looking for a loyal, affectionate companion who will soak up every moment by your side, Shalla is ready to finally be someone’s “baby.”

Toot too too toot! Trumpets blare! All hail, the grand "Duke" of Minnesota! Prepare yourself to meet this majestic boy, as he truly deserves the royal treatment.

Duke came into rescue with bones sticking out, porcupine quills sticking in, and a wish for the finer things in life - like a soft bed and a good squeaky ball! He has since put on some weight, glossed out his coat, and had a bunch of quills removed. He also gets those finer things!

Just like any "royal", Duke wants his voice to be heard, and will let you know his opinions on being left out of important family meetings, play sessions, or the neighbor's disc golf game. He is vocal and should not go to a home with shared walls.

Duke gets along great with the resident dogs, and enjoys playing with the resident pups (25lb 9mo male, 75lb 6yo female) and mostly ignores the cats, although he can use the occasional reminder they are friends.

Duke, like any regal person, likes his fancy house and does not destroy things when left out during the day, and is way too smart to potty inside. He does know "kennel", "sit", and "paw" and is doing really well learning "stay".

Duke does ok on walks, sometimes reacting to other dogs with his big bark, but is pretty easily corrected. However, with how big and strong he is, he could use some help learning even better manners. He is super treat motivated, so this should be easy!

Duke is a full-body snuggler - he's pretty convinced he's a lap dog! He enjoys movie nights just as much as play time, and is a nice medium energy level.

If Duke sounds like a match, make a request for an audience (meet and greet) with his lordship today!

Wilde & Lightfoot – Bonded Brothers Looking for a Patient Forever Home

Sometimes two is better than one, and that is definitely the case with these bonded brothers!

When Wilde and Lightfoot first came into rescue, they were very stressed and unsure of the world around them. Since then, they have been slowly learning to trust humans and discovering just how wonderful love and affection can be. Wilde is the more confident of the pair and helps show his brother, Lightfoot, that people can be safe and kind.

Once they feel comfortable, both boys enjoy pets and attention. Lightfoot has even been known to give sweet little kisses! Their bond is truly special—when they once thought they had been separated, both became anxious, and Lightfoot called out with loud meows until Wilde found him and could curl up beside him again.

These boys love spending time together, whether they’re sharing a meal of wet food, lounging in boxes, or relaxing in cozy cat perches and hideaways. They are excellent with their litter box and have wonderful house manners.

Wilde and Lightfoot will need a patient adopter who understands that trust takes time. A quieter home where they can settle in at their own pace would be ideal. In return, you’ll get the privilege of watching two deserving cats blossom and share their love with you—and with each other.

Their journey has taken time, but we’re confident these sweet brothers will become amazing companions for the lucky family who gives them the forever home they deserve. ❤️🐾

She also enjoys “supervising” her foster mom in the kitchen to make sure everything is running smoothly.

Marina is a happy, affectionate pup who will thrive in a home ready to continue her training, enjoy her playful side, and give her plenty of love (and cozy blankets).

Hi, I'm Gerald! (The Gentle Giant)

I am a super sweet, all-around good boy. People tell me I am just a little puppy trapped inside a

big dog body. I know the big scar above my left eye is the first thing you notice when you look at

me. You might assume a big boy like me was a top dog in dog fighting or used as a bait dog, but

that is the furthest from the truth. The story behind my scar might break your heart, but I can

promise you it hasn’t broken my spirit. I was starved of love in my past, which just means I am making up for lost time and am ready to receive all the love you have to give!

I am still learning how to be a real dog, and I am finding out that life is actually pretty amazing. I

am as easy to please as he is to love!

My Ideal Home:

- Kid-Friendly: I am excellent with smaller kids. However, I truly do not know my own strength. On rare occasions, I can get a little too rambunctious, so keep an eye on me around the little ones.

- Dog-Friendly: I am fantastic with other dogs and great with puppies, though my foster parent suggests monitoring puppy playtime.

- Pack Style: I can be your only dog, but I would absolutely thrive with a large alpha dog to look up to. I am definitely not an alpha male myself.

- Daily Flow: I am completely house-trained. I will vocalize and let you know when I need to go potty or when I am ready to play!

My Absolute Favorite Things:

- The Outdoors: I love being outside feeling the fresh air.

- New Games: I recently realized that playing tug-of-war is actually a game, and guess what? I am really good at it!

- Toys: I love balls of all kinds, squeaky or silent. I also adore carrying and chewing my stuffies.

- Food: Food is life! I will do just about anything for a tasty treat. Warning: I learn very fast exactly where the treat cupboard is located.

My One Little Fear:

- Please don't spray me with the garden hose on full blast. It scares me, so let's stick to gentle splashes instead.

Meet Mariska! ❤️

Mariska has been through more than most dogs ever should, but every day she's learning that people can be kind, love can be permanent, and home is a safe place. In 2025, we rescued a litter of her puppies, but we were finally able to rescue her this year. After spending so much of her life surviving and raising babies, this sweet mama is ready to retire, relax, and let someone take care of her for a change.

She's embracing indoor life and making incredible progress. Mariska has discovered the joy of toys and is wonderfully goofy when she plays—leaping, hopping, tossing toys into the air, and catching them herself. She's also incredibly smart! She already knows how to ring the bells to go outside, kennel on cue, sit, leave it, and she's eager to keep learning.

Once Mariska trusts you, she becomes the sweetest little shadow, happily following her favorite person from room to room. A few tasty treats, gentle face scratches, and belly rubs are the fastest way to win her heart. At the end of the day, she's happiest curled up beside you, handing out cuddles and plenty of kisses.

Mariska also enjoys the company of other dogs. She has become friends with every foster dog she's met, but she needs slow, thoughtful introductions and usually takes a day or two to feel comfortable. Because of this, she'd do best in a home that won't expect her to enjoy busy dog parks or daycare settings.

Due to her past, Mariska needs a patient adopter who understands that trust takes time. New people can still be frightening for her, and if she's approached too quickly, she may react out of fear. Her foster family is helping her build confidence, but she'll need someone committed to continuing that journey. For now, she would do best in a home without young children.

When Mariska first entered rescue, she struggled with separation anxiety, barking in her kennel, pawing at the door, or pulling nearby items inside for comfort. She has made tremendous progress, though it's possible some of those behaviors may temporarily return as she adjusts to a new home.

Mariska is looking for someone who will see past her fears and appreciate the incredible dog she's becoming. If you're willing to give her patience, consistency, and unconditional love, she'll reward you with unwavering loyalty, endless affection, and the joy of watching a once-forgotten dog truly blossom into the companion she was always meant to be. ❤️

Clancy is a perfect mix of enthusiasm, zoomies and love. He’s full of boundless energy and curiosity about the world, but also loves to snuggle on the couch giving kisses to his favorite humans.

He is absolutely the cutest dog - he gets compliments everywhere he goes. That floppy ear is just too cute! And his fur is as soft as it looks.

Clancy loves being outdoors (except in the rain.) He loves a good walk and his leash skills are improving. A good game of fetch is his favorite activity - he loves anything with a slightly unpredictable bounce. A fenced backyard would be great for Clancy.

But, he also makes a great office mate. He loves to hang out in the office while a person is on calls, as long as he has toys to chew. He truly just loves to be where is his people are.

Wrestling is another favorite pastime. He’s full of puppy energy and loves wrestling with our young adult German Shepherd - it’s a great way to wear him out. He is a bit much for older or more timid dogs.

Clancy has done well with all the kids he has met, enjoying the belly rubs and kisses. He sees them as small people - and he just adores people! He does show his excitement through jumping, so he might be best with slightly older kids.

He spends the night in the crate and chunks of the daytime, as needed. But really, he wants to be where the people are! He’s happy to follow people from room to room - and he doesn’t let a baby gate get in his way.

Time in a crate may be important for Clancy. He tends to get super excited about life, and a little alone time with a Kong helps him settle back down. He typically complains for 1-2 minutes, but quickly quiets.

This puppy is SUPER food motivated! We haven’t found a food or treat he doesn’t like. This has been helpful in training him to sit and lay down. He can also stay for very brief chunks of time - but he’s still a curious and impulsive puppy who just wants to be with his people, so that will take more work.

Clancy does have a fairly strong prey drive. He’s curious about birds, rabbits and squirrels, which is where a fenced yard is handy. He sometimes sees cats and smaller (or more timid) dogs as prey, so it’s best to be in a cat-free home. A confident dog can help redirect him, but he’s also a bit picky about his furry companions.

Other Clancy strengths: potty trained, kennel trained, not destructive outside the crate, loves to play, master of slobbery kisses

Alcatraz (we call her Ali) came to our home as a very scared, timid and skinny little girl who survived parvo and was simply looking for love. She has come a long way since then as she has discovered people are not scary and she will greet you with tail wags that actually move her entire body. She is learning boundaries because her puppy excitement causes her to, well, be a puppy which means jumping and mouthing, but she is a fast learner and, ultimately, all Ali wants to do is please you. Alcatraz is a quiet dog who rarely barks except when she is about to get the zoomies or when you talk to her and try to get a conversation going. It's quite priceless.

Alcatraz can sit before she gets food and sits on command. She is working on potty training due to being a parvo puppy who was inside for much of her early life. She is food motivated and has shown her ability to get to food no matter how high it is placed. She loves toys and safe chews and is not destructive at all. Playing with other dogs is one of her favorite things to do as running is second nature to her and she is definitely an Olympic qualifier in that sport. Currently, she spends part of the day in a wire kennel with a cover on it and does well. No barking or whining as long as she has something to play with inside it. She also doesn't like a messy kennel and has kept hers clean even if she is in it for more than four hours.

This little girl has won our hearts not only because she is beautiful with hazel eyes and ears that need their own zip code, but also because she is so excited to be safe and loved that she will be a loyal dog to whomever is lucky enough to become her forever family. Could this be you?

Introducing Zephyr! This boy is an absolute gem. He is ready to leave his past behind and start a new adventure with his very own family. While he is still learning about indoor life, for probably the first time, he has made great strides. Because the inside world is totally new, little things like hard floors, brooms, mops, and unfamiliar noises are a bit scary for him, but he gets a little braver every day. I do not have stairs in my home so I am unsure of how he does with them.  A calm, lower traffic home that can offer patience and a lot of love would suit him best.

While in foster, Zephyr has learned that a soft bed is the best! Toys? Yes, please; but make them sturdy! The joy of destroying a stuffy is not lost on this guy! He loves to chew his Kong ball and his Benebone. The concept of fetch is not quite understood right now. He’s more of a run after the ball and then look back at you with a “now what” face. With practice, he will get there.

Currently, the leash is very scary for him. He is working on wearing it while inside. Because he is treat motivated, he is working on step, treat, step, treat. Patience and time with definitely get him there.

Zephyr has done slow introductions with dogs and has done well with calm dogs. He will need time to adjust to his new home before dog interactions can happen.

After a few days of getting comfortable and building trust, his true personality begins to show. Playing in the yard is a favorite. He runs, watches the birds and the cats, lounges in the sun and loves to sit by his people for belly rubs. You can tell he is genuinely happy. For his safety, a fenced yard is best. He will thrive in a home where he can experience love, be shown patience to learn and most importantly, spend a lot of time with his people.

Zephyr is potty trained, kennel trained, has a medium energy level, quiet…all around perfect guy!

Are you the kind of person that likes to see the magic happen? Are you wanting a rewarding experience and a truly grateful friend? This is your guy! If you are patient and willing to put in the effort, Zephyr will make you smile and amaze you every day. He is patiently waiting to impress you! Fill out that application, you will be happy you did!

Meet Cumulo/Storm! 🌩️🤍

This is Storm! (the name she has caught onto and goes by in her foster home). If you're looking for a puppy who absolutely adores people, is eager to learn, is super smart, and has the sweetest heart, Storm might be your perfect match.

Storm has never met a person she didn't want to love. She greets everyone with a wagging tail, a happy smile, and plenty of kisses. More than anything, she wants to be with her people. Whether it's following you around the house, curling up nearby, or sitting patiently at your feet waiting for pets or her next cue, she is happiest when she's part of the action.

One of our favorite things about Storm is just how smart she is. She genuinely wants to learn. She has already mastered "sit" and "lay down," and she frequently offers those behaviors on her own, sitting politely at our feet without even being asked. She listens incredibly well for such a young puppy and picks up new routines and expectations amazingly fast. She's the kind of dog who will absolutely thrive with someone excited to continue teaching her.

Storm also has an incredible nose and loves using it! She is a focused little detective who enjoys tracking scents and investigating the world around her. Watching her use her nose is one of our favorite things because you can see how much she enjoys the process of figuring things out. We think Storm is the kind of dog who could truly thrive with a “job"- whether that means scent work, structured training, search-and-rescue-style activities, another enrichment activity, or simply being a wonderful companion for someone who loves teaching and engaging with their dog. She isn't just a puppy who wants attention; she wants to learn, solve problems, and have a purpose. A home that is ready to exercise both her body and her beautiful brain will help Storm become the amazing dog we already know she is becoming.

For a puppy, Storm has an impressively nice "off switch." She can absolutely romp and wrestle with the best of them, but when playtime is over, she's happy to relax and simply hang out with her people. She isn't constantly bouncing off the walls; in fact, we've been continually impressed by how calm she can be once her energy is out. She loves having fun, but she also loves just being near her people.

Storm is also doing wonderfully with her crate routine. She typically settles into her crate around 9:30 p.m. and sleeps all the way until about 5:30 to 6:00 a.m. She may give a brief little whine once in a while, but it doesn't last long, and she settles herself quickly.

She's dog-friendly and loves having canine friends. Like many puppies, she's still learning that not every dog wants to play quite as enthusiastically as she does! She and her foster brother sometimes get a little carried away during play, but she's taking corrections beautifully and has made tremendous progress in a short amount of time. She doesn't really "puppy bite" people, but she does like to use her foster brother as her chew toy and is learning when that isn't appropriate or when play has gone too far. She clearly loves playing, and she's learning better manners every day. She is also making progress learning to "just be" around other dogs and enjoy their company… it is hard because she loves so hard, but she continues to make progress every day.

Storm has also lived around cats. In a previous foster home, she mostly ignored them. In our home, she's become very excited to make feline friends. She has absolutely no desire to hurt them, she simply wants to rush over, say hello, and cover them in kisses, which, understandably, not every cat appreciates! She's making great progress with guidance, but if her future home has cats, we'd recommend cats who are confident and dog-savvy while she continues learning polite introductions. A home with cats who are already comfortable around dogs and understand puppy enthusiasm would likely be the best fit.

Hi, I’m Duffy and I’d love to meet you! I’m a peppy pup, who also has moments of chill! One of my favorite things to do is hang out in my foster mom’s backyard. Sometimes I like to patrol the perimeter, sniffing as I go. Other times I like to run zoomies or just prance around doing my deer imitations (foster mom here…sometimes he kind of bounds instead of running or walking..it is the cutest thing!) or just hang out on the stoop and see what’s going on in the neighborhood.

Foster mom says I should tell you that I have not had single accident in the house…I prefer to do my business on a bush or grass! Earlier on she put me in this thing called a crate. She lured me in with some treats…I am pretty food motivated. I wasn’t a huge fan and generally would express my displeasure with whining & the husky howl for a little bit before settling down. After a few days, foster mom experimented with not crating me when she left and her trust was not misplaced…I have not chewed on anything besides dog toys! I do love bones & antlers & hard toys, but I also have fun with a little stuffed pink pig! I might have gotten into more trouble if she hadn’t put all the food away…I do like my food!

I tend to follow foster mom around the house to keep an eye on what’s she’s doing. She did say that I’m not yet a great snuggler…I love pets when I’m standing around, but if I get up on the sofa with her, I have trouble sitting still. I do enjoy when new people stop by & I can get some pets from them, although it may take me a moment to make sure they are safe.

I live with another, bigger dog. When I first met him, I was a bit nervous, so foster mom did what she calls a ‘slow intro’. Now we like to hang out in the backyard together and sometimes we run zoomies together. Foster mom says to tell you she thinks I could live with another dog, but that I certainly don’t need a brother or sister.

So that’s me! I’ve you have questions or want to learn more, put in an application & my foster mom can tell you more!

Meet your new best friend, Kaya!

This sweet girl may be a little timid when you first meet her, but once she feels safe, her loving personality shines. Earn her trust, and you'll have a loyal companion who's happiest right by your side—especially if she can do her signature "lean" against your leg while you give her pets.

She's house-trained, kennel-trained, and such a well-mannered girl that she doesn't really need to be crated. At the end of the day, she's an early-to-bed kind of gal who loves curling up in a cozy, cave-like space for a good night's sleep.

She's wonderful with other dogs, especially smaller ones, and would be happy with a canine companion or as your one-and-only. One way to her heart? Cheese! It's her absolute favorite treat.

Like many dogs, loud noises such as fireworks and motorcycles can be scary for her, so she'll do best with a family that understands her need for a calm, reassuring environment during noisy moments.

If you're looking for a gentle soul who will reward your patience with unwavering love and loyalty, this girl is ready to become your devoted best friend. Give her a little time to settle in, and she'll give you her whole heart.

When Harold first came to my home, he was incredibly quiet and clearly scared of the world around him. In fact, I had to carry him up the three steps to get inside because it didn't even seem like he knew what stairs were. The saddest part was watching him try to adjust to a home environment. While my dogs were curled up on the couches enjoying warm, comfortable naps, Harold just paced and paced, constantly wanting to go back outside. He had likely spent so much time in a loud, busy shelter that the peace and quiet of a home felt unfamiliar. Everything around him was new—the soft couches, the calm atmosphere, the simple comfort of feeling safe.

The next day, something changed. It was just Harold and me inside while the other dogs were outside playing. I was sitting on the couch doing my own thing and letting him explore at his own pace. After a while, he quietly jumped up onto the couch, looked out the window for a few moments, and then settled down right next to me. Before long, he was fast asleep. For the first time, it seemed like he finally realized he could let his guard down. He had found a safe, quiet place to rest his head, and he slept there for hours.

Since then, Harold's personality has slowly started to shine through. While he may be a bigger dog, he is truly the gentlest soul. He loves playing with his foster siblings, soaking up affection, and sitting beside you on the couch just to be close. He sleeps wonderfully in his kennel, is almost fully potty trained, and has the sweetest, calmest demeanor. Harold doesn't ask for much—just a little patience, kindness, and a place to belong. Beneath his gentle eyes is a dog who has learned to trust again, and watching him discover what it means to be loved has been one of the most rewarding experiences. Harold is proof that sometimes the softest hearts come in the biggest packages.

Meet a dog who embodies everything people love about a presumed Husky mix—adventurous, intelligent, full of personality, and always ready for the next adventure. This energetic pup approaches life with enthusiasm and curiosity, whether it’s exploring a new trail, playing with favorite toys, or simply finding a fun way to entertain himself. His sharp mind makes him a quick learner, though that independent Husky streak means he sometimes likes to decide for himself when and how things should be done.

Friendly and social, this dog enjoys being around people and thrives on companionship. He can be vocal and expressive, never shy about sharing his thoughts, feelings, or excitement with his family. As a hardworking and active companion, he’ll do best with a home that can provide plenty of physical exercise and mental enrichment to keep his busy mind engaged.

Like many Husky mixes, he can be a little mischievous when boredom strikes and he doesn’t enjoy being left alone for long periods. He would prefer being part of the action and spending time with his people. Toys are his favorite pastime, and he loves having outlets for his energy and playful nature. This pup also prefers not to be kenneled and would thrive with a family committed to helping him succeed through structure, activity, and companionship.

Because of his instinctual prey drive and preferences, he is looking for a cat-free home. For the right family, Joss will be a loyal adventure partner, a source of daily laughter, and a constant reminder that life is meant to be lived to the fullest.

Meet Puck! ❤️

If you're looking for a loyal cuddle buddy, Puck might just be your perfect match. This sweet, gentle boy is happiest when he's close to his people, soaking up all the love and giving plenty of kisses in return. While he's quiet and well-behaved in his kennel, he'd much rather be curled up beside you on the couch.

Puck enjoys going for walks and does fairly well on a leash, though he can get excited and pull from time to time. He also loooooves car rides and is always happy to hop in for an adventure! One of his most irresistible features is his big, fluffy face—he absolutely loves having his squishy cheeks rubbed!

He's not very food motivated, but he does know how to sit and is eager to spend time with his people. Puck is both house-trained and kennel-trained, making him an easy companion to welcome into your home.

Puck is currently living in a foster home with cats. While he's been manageable with training and commands, his breed tends to have a higher prey drive, so he would do best in a home without cats.

Like many happy dogs, Puck may jump up occasionally when he's excited to see you, but it's all out of love. Puck is also scheduled for TPLO surgery on 7/17. He'll need a family committed to supporting him through his recovery, and once he's healed, he'll be ready to enjoy all the adventures and snuggles life has to offer.

If you're looking for a sweet, affectionate companion who will happily trade endless cuddles, kisses, and car rides for a loving home, Puck is ready to meet you!

He was uncertain on his first day in foster care, but he’s already made great progress. As he settles in, his curiosity is starting to shine and his personality is coming out more each day.

Belva is an absolute dream with children of all ages. She has a beautifully balanced energy level—she is always down for an exciting neighborhood walk, but the second you step inside, she transitions seamlessly into a professional napper.

Her Superpowers:

- House-Trained Champion: She is fully house-trained and hasn't had a single accident!

- Crate-Trained Pro: She settles beautifully and loves her own cozy space.

- Eager Learner: She is highly motivated by treats and loves working on her basic commands.

Her Ideal Home:

While Belva is a rockstar with humans, the big wide world can sometimes feel a bit overwhelming for her. She gets nervous around other dogs, so she would thrive best as the only pet in the home. She is ready to pour 100% of her love, loyalty, and affection directly into her human family.

If you want a smart, gentle, family-loving pup who is already past the difficult house-training phase, Belva is the girl for you!

Augusto, a.k.a. Trouble, is the instigator of the litter. He likes to get his siblings all riled up and simply walk away to go find a stick or just lay in the grass and watch the chaos unfold. He loves toys, chasing feet, chewies, and sniffing the clovers looking for bugs to chase or left over sniffle mat snacks! He responds to Trouble and has started to come when called. Once he realizes he has left the group he comes running back! Trouble has had the most difficulty with kennel training and does not like being in there. After about 5 minutes he usually calms down and takes his nap or goes to bed. He also needs to be watched for fast sniffing to go potty because he hasn’t figured out a way to signal yet that he needs to go. 🐾 Meet Wojapi ("Javi") 🖤🤍

If you've been looking for the perfect little sidekick, your search might just be over.

Wojapi—better known as Javi in his foster home—is one of the smartest, cuddliest puppies you'll ever meet. This sweet boy has a serious case of FOMO, which means his favorite place in the world is wherever you are. Whether he's snuggled in your lap, sleeping on your feet, or wrestling with his furry siblings, Javi just wants to be part of the action.

He absolutely adores other dogs and has gained so much confidence from his foster siblings. Because of that, we'd love to see him join a home with another playful, confident pup who can continue showing him the ropes.

Despite being just a few weeks old, Javi is already impressing everyone! He:

🐾 Knows "sit"

🐾 Is doing great with potty training and improving every day

🐾 Sleeps through the night like a champ

🐾 Loves exploring the outdoors, rolling in the grass, and going on walks

Like any young puppy, Javi is still learning that being alone is okay. He'll need a family committed to continuing his training so he can grow into a confident, independent adult.

If you're looking for a loyal shadow, an adventure buddy, and a professional cuddle bug all wrapped up in one adorable puppy, Javi is ready to steal your heart.

Opal is a tiny warrior wrapped in the softest, sweetest soul. After spending over a week in the hospital fighting parvo, she beat the odds with quiet determination. Now she’s learning how to enjoy the world again — slowly, softly, and at her own pace.

This mellow little pup wants connection so badly, but eye contact can still feel a bit overwhelming. She’ll peek at you, look away, then try again. Her bravery comes in small, precious steps.

Now that she’s feeling better, Opal has discovered two great joys in life: food and soft squeaky toys. She’s also the cleanest puppy her foster has ever met. While she’s still working on potty training, she does wonderfully with potty pads.

Opal isn’t a fan of baths, but she adores soft, fuzzy pajamas — especially when the air conditioning feels chilly. She’s a comfort‑seeker at heart.

Opal is searching for a patient, gentle home that celebrates tiny victories and understands that courage grows slowly. She may not be fully brave yet, but she’s trying every day, and she’ll blossom beautifully with someone who lets her move at her own pace.

Meet Carlotta—or Lottie to her friends!

This beautiful Shepherd mix is finally ready for a life that's all about her. After raising multiple litters of puppies, Lottie has more than earned her happily-ever-after. She's now spayed and looking for a family who will shower her with the love and care she's always deserved.

Lottie absolutely adores people and is happiest when she's by your side, soaking up affection and attention. She's friendly with other dogs and enjoys having canine companions, but true to her Shepherd roots, she can be a little bossy and likes to herd her dog friends from time to time. She'll do best with dogs that don't mind her taking charge every now and then.

One of Lottie's most charming features is her adorable ear—one stands tall while the other flops off to the side, giving her the sweetest, slightly goofy expression that everyone falls in love with.

She's a joy to walk on a leash, knows some basic commands, and is a wonderful companion for neighborhood strolls or relaxing at home. She's smart, eager to please, and loves spending time with her people.

Lottie does have a high prey drive, so any home with cats would need to have a very dog-savvy, confident cat, and slow, thoughtful introductions would be essential. She may also be happiest in a home without small animals.

After spending years taking care of everyone else, it's finally Lottie's turn to be loved. She's ready for a family who will cherish her, appreciate her loyal heart, laugh at her adorable sideways ear, and give her the forever home she's been waiting for.

Could Lottie be the missing piece of your family?

Hey everyone! I’m Henry!  I’m known for my big beautiful ears, white coat, and an even bigger personality.

I’m food motivated and enjoy being close to my people. I don’t nip, but will jump up to kiss you!

I need a confident and patient home to build my confidence and help me learn it’s okay to relax and settle in. I prefer sleeping with or by you as I treat my crate as storage for my toys and use it for meal time! Every time I play with a toy, I end up bringing it into the crate. I’m not totally sure about the roomba going off in the morning, so I run to my crate and sit in there until it’s over! I’m slowly learning the crate is a good safe space. Food is one of the best things to me, so it’s best to keep it out of my view as my sniffers are always on trying to search for some crumbs.

I do best with a large space where I can get plenty of exercise, enrichment, and be vocal to help me burn energy and continue learning during this stage. On walks I am learning that if my foster parents stop in their tracks and calls for me, that I should come and sit for a treat!

I’ve been around small pets and don’t mind them too much with supervision. With proper and slow introductions, I may be able to coexist with a companion dog. I’m still in potty training and doing well with structure and routine. I’ve started picking up that if I paw at the front door, I am in need of doing by business outdoors.

I'm a curious puppy with big potential as I’m still young! If you know someone looking for a sidekick with oversized ears and a whole lot of personality, send them my way!

Woof! 🐶

MEET PECAN: SMALL BOY. BIG EARS. EVEN BIGGER AUDACITY.

Pecan is just puppy and has already figured out the secret to getting away with absolutely everything: sad puppy eyes and ridiculously big ears.

Did he do something naughty? Probably not.

Were there witnesses? Irrelevant.

Just look into those sad little eyes and tell him he’s guilty. YOU CAN’T. CASE DISMISSED.

Pecan is a friendly, playful little guy who is good with kids and other dogs and is always ready to make a new friend. His hobbies include playing, being adorable, eating snacks, thinking about snacks, and wondering if you happen to have any snacks. He is VERY food and treat motivated, so the way to his heart is pretty straightforward.

He’s still working on potty training because, excuse you, he is just a baby. He’s doing his best and would appreciate it if we focused on his many other talents… like being cute.

As for the crate? Pecan would like to formally announce his displeasure IMMEDIATELY. There may be some crying. There may be a brief protest. He may act as though he has been sentenced to life without parole. But don’t worry—once he realizes his legal team isn’t coming, he settles down.

Pecan is looking for a forever family who will give him lots of love, playtime, patience, treats, and the occasional reminder that being ridiculously adorable does not mean the rules don’t apply to him.

Although… with those eyes and ears?

Yeah. Good luck with that.

Come meet Pecan and prepare to fall in love with this sweet little nut.

She LOVES people and must be near them, otherwise she can become highly anxious if left alone. The one night she was kenneled, she tried to escape (hence, the scratched up snout in the photos).

Meet Zoey: The Sweetest Little Soul You’ll Ever Have the Privilege of Knowing

Zoey’s story didn’t begin the way she deserved. This tiny 5lb. senior Chihuahua came to rescue blind, frightened, and carrying the weight of a life that wasn’t always kind. But every day, she’s showing us something remarkable: the incredible resilience of a dog who has decided to trust again.

Zoey isn’t looking for adventure, she’s looking for peace.

Her favorite things are soft blankets, cozy naps, constant pets, and simply being close to the people she loves. Once she feels safe, she’ll happily melt into your arms or curl up beside you for hours. Her little tail has started wagging more often, she's following her nose when the scent of food is around, and every sign of trust feels like a victory worth celebrating.

Being blind doesn’t slow Zoey down nearly as much as you’d think. She learns her surroundings quickly, follows familiar voices, and navigates life with her nose, her ears, and a quiet confidence that grows a more every day. Now that she has had her dental needs cared for as well, she is feeling healthy and strong! All she asks is a predictable home and someone patient enough to let her discover that she’s finally truly safe.

Zoey would thrive in a calm home where she can enjoy the simple things in life. She’s well past the puppy stage, past the chaos, and past the need to prove anything. She simply wants to spend her golden years being cherished.

If you’ve ever believed that love isn’t measured in years but in moments, Zoey may be the dog you’ve been waiting for. She has spent enough of her life surviving. Now she’s ready to spend the rest of it being someone’s beloved little shadow.

Messi definitely had a hard start. Our best guess is that he was kept in a kennel with his brother for most of his life, before being surrendered to rescue. Also, that he was kept outside because of the bug bites on his ears. It has been such an extraordinary joy to watch him come out of his shell and bound around our yard and house, full of joy. It is as if we are watching him discover every single sweet thing that life has to offer him as he experiences it for the first time!

We have been working on helping Messi gain a bit of weight, increases muscle mass, and have been giving him soothing baths to heal his skin and get his healthy coat back. He has some sores on his skin and feet that are now healing. Regardless of what he has been through, he is the happiest boy! Once his healing is complete, he is going to be even more beautiful dog than he is now. And we think he is a looker already! Our best guest on breed combination is: some sort of small terrier breed/cattle dog/labrador retriever/Staffordshire terrier/husky/shepherd and likely others. He is a beautiful little mix of things with the greatest personality and disposition.

Messi is already past his shark teeth phase and has all of his adult teeth. He is extremely gentle when he uses his mouth. He eats quickly now that he has access to food, but is gentle with people around food and treats/bones/toys. No resource guarding so far. And, he’s at a beautiful age to bond with his new people, just as he has done with us.

Messi at his present age and weight is projected to unlikely become a large dog. He is a perfectly-perfect size. He currently lives with our dogs, cats, and three children aged 9- to 16-years-old and is so wonderful and respectful with all of them!

He has had absolutely no accidents in the temp foster home he was in before ours, or in our home. He prefers to go to the bathroom outside, and does so immediately when taking there and given the “potty” command. Of course, we give him ample opportunities to potty outside-at least one every couple hours.

He also responds to “come”, “sit”, “no”, and is working on “kennel”. Also working on leash training, and doing great, although he is also great off-leash and has good recall. He loves to be right by his people. He rides wonderfully in a car outside of, or in, a kennel. Messi is a very fast learner. He lives to please his people. He sleeps happily kenneled next to his foster canine companions all night from 10pm to 6am with no accidents and not a peep.

Messi is a very loving, happy, joyful, laid-back, AFFECTIONATE, grateful, playful boy who loves being near his people. He really lives for the acceptance and love of his humans. If his people are not around, he finds comfort in his canine companions and would benefit from joining a family with another confident, friendly dog in it, to show him the ropes and be friends with. Messi does not enjoy being alone, as he was with his brother for his entire life before coming to our home.

Messi is looking for his forever family to start his journey with. Might that be yours!?

She absolutely loves to play with other dogs and adores any attention that comes her way.  If you let her, she’ll come up and sit right in your lap.

She is very food focused, and doesn’t like to share, so it’s best to feed her away from pets that may want to come and share her bowl with her.  Even though she is really good with other dogs, we think it’s best that she isn’t in a house with small pets as she does seem to target them with unwanted attention.

She’s been an absolute joy to have around and is very good natured and lovable.  Any household will be lucky to have her! The first thing you’ll notice about her, though are her eyes. No picture can do justice to how beautiful her eyes are..

Rescue Network is strictly foster based which means all adoptable animals are in a home and there cannot be a meet until an application is submitted and approved. We have no way of knowing the breeds in the animals. All breeds listed are best guess.
